---
title: "Low level vs high level visual prediction processing"
author: "Max Van Migem"
date: "2025-08-26"
---

```{r packages}
# Load required packages
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(parallel)
library(tidyverse)

```
```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Function to simulate LME data
simulate_lme_data <- function(n_subjects, n_timepoints, 
                             fixed_intercept, fixed_slope,
                             subject_intercept_sd, subject_slope_sd,
                             residual_sd, effect_size = 0) {
  
  # Generate subject-level random effects
  subjects <- data.frame(
    subject_id = 1:n_subjects,
    subject_intercept = rnorm(n_subjects, 0, subject_intercept_sd),
    subject_slope = rnorm(n_subjects, 0, subject_slope_sd)
  )
  
  # Create longitudinal data
  data <- expand.grid(
    subject_id = 1:n_subjects,
    time = 1:n_timepoints
  ) %>%
    left_join(subjects, by = "subject_id") %>%
    mutate(
      # Fixed effects + random effects + noise
      y = fixed_intercept + subject_intercept + 
          (fixed_slope + effect_size + subject_slope) * time +
          rnorm(n(), 0, residual_sd),
      # Group variable (for between-subjects effects)
      group = ifelse(subject_id <= n_subjects/2, "control", "treatment"),
      # Time as factor for categorical analysis
      time_factor = as.factor(time)
    )
  
  return(data)
}
```
```{r}
# Function to run single simulation
run_simulation <- function(sim_id, n_subjects, n_timepoints, 
                          fixed_intercept, fixed_slope,
                          subject_intercept_sd, subject_slope_sd,
                          residual_sd, effect_size) {
  
  # Simulate data
  sim_data <- simulate_lme_data(
    n_subjects, n_timepoints, fixed_intercept, fixed_slope,
    subject_intercept_sd, subject_slope_sd, residual_sd, effect_size
  )
  
  tryCatch({
    # Fit LME model - testing time effect
    model <- lmer(y ~ time + (1 + time | subject_id), data = sim_data)
    
    # Extract p-value for time effect
    summary_model <- summary(model)
    p_value <- summary_model$coefficients["time", "Pr(>|t|)"]
    
    # Return results
    return(data.frame(
      sim_id = sim_id,
      p_value = p_value,
      significant = p_value < 0.05,
      converged = TRUE
    ))
  }, error = function(e) {
    return(data.frame(
      sim_id = sim_id,
      p_value = NA,
      significant = FALSE,
      converged = FALSE
    ))
  })
}

# Run Monte Carlo simulation
monte_carlo_lme_power <- function(n_simulations = 1000, n_subjects = 50, 
                                 n_timepoints = 5, effect_size = 0.5,
                                 fixed_intercept = 10, fixed_slope = 0.2,
                                 subject_intercept_sd = 2, subject_slope_sd = 0.5,
                                 residual_sd = 1, n_cores = detectCores() - 1) {
  
  # Run simulations in parallel
  if (n_cores > 1) {
    cl <- makeCluster(n_cores)
    clusterEvalQ(cl, {
      library(lme4)
      library(lmerTest)
    })
    
    results <- parLapply(cl, 1:n_simulations, function(i) {
      run_simulation(i, n_subjects, n_timepoints, fixed_intercept, fixed_slope,
                    subject_intercept_sd, subject_slope_sd, residual_sd, effect_size)
    })
    
    stopCluster(cl)
  } else {
    results <- lapply(1:n_simulations, function(i) {
      run_simulation(i, n_subjects, n_timepoints, fixed_intercept, fixed_slope,
                    subject_intercept_sd, subject_slope_sd, residual_sd, effect_size)
    })
  }
  
  # Combine results
  results_df <- bind_rows(results)
  
  # Calculate power
  power <- mean(results_df$significant, na.rm = TRUE)
  convergence_rate <- mean(results_df$converged, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  return(list(
    power = power,
    convergence_rate = convergence_rate,
    results = results_df,
    parameters = list(
      n_simulations = n_simulations,
      n_subjects = n_subjects,
      n_timepoints = n_timepoints,
      effect_size = effect_size
    )
  ))
}

# Example usage
# Run simulation with default parameters
power_results <- monte_carlo_lme_power(
  n_simulations = 100,  # Use more simulations for final analysis (e.g., 1000)
  n_subjects = 50,
  n_timepoints = 5,
  effect_size = 0.5
)

cat(sprintf("Power: %.3f\n", power_results$power))
cat(sprintf("Convergence rate: %.3f\n", power_results$convergence_rate))
```
